## 8. 5 Rules To Become a Successful Entrepreneur

most business advice on YouTube is BS
everyone is telling you you have to wake
up at 4:00 a.m. work 16 hours and never
go see your family in the holidays the
truth is last year my ADC did $23
million in Revenue I pick up my kids
every day from school I put them to
sleep and I live a extremely happy well
balanced life so in this video I'm going
to tell you five rules for you to follow
so then you can become a more successful
entrepreneur so the first rule is don't
compare yourself to others as we know
comparison is the thief of joy and the
truth truth is today on social media it
is so easy to sit there and compare your
success and how you are currently
feeling doing and every single thing to
somebody else you see online and this
exact thing was the thing that really I
struggled with but I first started out

## 10. 5 rules I learnt that made me $1m (147 times)

Look, the truth is, if you ever want to
get to seven figures plus year, there's
some fundamental things that you're
going to have to change about yourself.
You almost have to forget the old you,
and you have to welcome the new you if
you ever want to get there. In this
video, I'm going to be breaking down the
five things you need to do to go from
where you are now to become a
seven-figure founder. Look, whether
you're a freelancer or you're a small
agency owner, or you're someone who's
early on the journey, you're going to
find this video super useful. And my
name is Sean, and on this channel, I
break down exactly how I built my
business Jamf to do a hundred million
dollars plus in revenue. And during that
time now, I work with some of the
biggest brands and creators in the
world. I also invest in businesses and
have a portfolio, which itself is doing
more than 50 million dollars this year.
So, all of that I do on this very
channel, but let's jump straight in. So,
the very first one is that you need to
do unscalable things. Yes, you heard me
right. Everybody gets this wrong. If I
asked you to sit and send 10-15 emails
per day, you give me that Yeah, but
that's not scalable. I can't get my
clients like that. If I asked you to sit
and respond to every DM you get, Sean,
that's not scalable. I can't be doing
that. Most people are so worried about
doing scalable things that they actually
don't scale their business. It's one of
the key reasons I was able to build my
business cuz every single time I'm like,
"What is the thing that's going to make
this thing work?" I don't really care if
it's manual, if it's even costly, if
it's boring. If you picked up your phone
right now, and you went to every
interaction you had on social media, and
you sent them a full video of you
speaking of breaking down the funnel and
telling them what you can do for them as
a business, it's hugely unscalable. I
don't have that time. I'm busy. I don't
want to be doing that, right? But that's
exactly why it'll work, and this is
actually why you'll win. You have to
actually do the unscalable things cuz
people are not willing to do that, and
that gives you a natural advantage. The
second thing is that you have to master
communication on calls. Most of our time
now is spent on calls. People ask me all
the time, "Sean, you know how am I meant
to sign like a really big client on a
call? It's just not really possible."
No, it's because you don't know how to
communicate well. So, one of the key
things on calls and being able to master
communication is you have to let the
other person feel like they have won.
Every person you speak with, whether
that's a pitch, whether that's a client,
whether that's someone you're trying to
hire, you've got to let them like feel
like they're in control of the
conversation. How you do that is by
allowing them to speak. How you do that
is by asking open-ended questions that
lets them shine for a second. By you
controlling how you are speaking and
what you're doing in a call is a massive
thing to be able to actually get what
you want out of calls, which leads to
success and growth. Related to that,
very important, you've got to change
your communication style based on who
you are speaking with. That's one of my
biggest secrets to go from starting to,
you know, a business that's doing eight
figures in revenue with a nine-figure
valuation, it's because of me changing
my communication style based on the
call. So, if I'm on a call, it's a
female creator, and you know, she's
under 25, I'm going to have to be a lot
more relaxed and not be so about money.
I won't reference people that are not
applicable to her because I'm realizing
in my communication style, in what I'm
saying and how I'm saying it, you have
to change it based on the audience.
Similarly, if I'm in a board meeting,
very different. I'm going to be very
professional. I've got to speak in a
certain way. I've got to use certain
buzzwords. I've got to present in a
certain way. I've got to show authority.
Very different. If I'm speaking with an
agency owner, I know what they know. So,
I'm going to say in that way. Maybe I'm
going to relax my approach a bit. The
point is, it's calculated. You have to
actually have a plan of who am I
speaking to, what am I going to say, and
how am I going to say it. If you break
that down and actually plan for the how
you're going to come across in phone
calls, it makes a massive difference.
Even now when I'm recording this video,
it's conscious that I'm not going to be
more aggravated and more aggressive. I'm

speak, you know, a bit more slowly, a
bit more relaxed, so you trust me more.
I'm not saying, "No, you guys have to do
this now. If you don't, you are not

to have it that style? This is exactly
how you need to be thinking when it

single biggest reason people sign bigger

because I understood that very early on
that I need to go and win the room. I

## 23. Building Neutonic with Chris Williamson and James Smith

are ready
for okay so for the past 12 months I
have been working on a new brand with
Chris Williamson and James Smith which
has just gone live it's called newtonic
it's the world's first productivity
drink we wanted to make something which
can truly help you focus and achieve
more in life and we believe we have done
it in this video I'm going to be taking
you through behind the scenes and how it
all came together over the past 12
months 12 months ago I remember sitting
to myself and thinking there's a massive
opportunity in the market that if we
could help people focus more get more
done procrastinate less this could be a
massive opportunity in the market and
during that time I ended up meeting
James Smith and then Chris and we
started thinking could we do this
together and the journey began and what
that meant was almost 12 months of phone
calls back and forwards trying to figure
out what what would be the best
ingredients scientifically that will
actually make that happen the problem
that we're trying to fix for customers
is that they feel like they're built for
more they feel like they should be able
to do more and there is something
getting in the way of them fulfilling
their potential it's like you're already
enough but we need to unlock that
potential and you can unlock that by
using the drink that my preference would
be a descriptive word which gives some
nod to what the end thing is in some way
on the way to train at 6 p.m. so I went
into a Servo and I was like oh I really
need something to pick me up cuz I'm
tired but I don't want to have too much
caffeine I can't sleep so I bought a
bottle and I actually became our
demographic for that kind of evening uh
pickup and it tasted like piss it was
the worst tasting thing I've ever had my
passion for energy drinks neut Tropics
as well for years I've almost been
self-medicated the desire to be
productive alert
cognitively my best we did a survey of
more than 15,000 of our combined
audience members about what they were
struggling with most and staying focused
having energy that doesn't crash and go
through highs and blows throughout the
day not getting distracted when they're
trying to stay on task like we made this
for those people like we took the
research and we ensured that this fixes
the problem that they have as far as I
can see there's nothing on the market
that actually does what we wanted it to

## 26. Business is hard until you build systems like this

Look, there's only two possible outcomes
for your business today. Either you're
going to build something that has real
value and you're able to sell it or
you're going to run your business and
ultimately one day it's going to close
down. And the truth is most people leave
it too late and they will not know what
they've done wrong. I know this
personally because I've built my
business Gymreapers to go from zero to a
hundred million dollars in revenue and
every single year we have grown because
of the systems that I have installed.
And look, the truth is I know exactly
how you feel because in the early years
I was the bottleneck. In the early years
I was not good at delegating. In the
early years I didn't know which systems
I had to have in place. So in this video
I'm going to break down the four key
systems that every business must have
based on my own learning of scaling my
own business which I think if you
install today you're going to end up
building a business that can scale
without you having to do every single
thing and ultimately build something of
value which you can exit one day. The
very first system that you need to
install is all about operations and this
is where you need to become a
McDonald's. So let me explain. Every
single McDonald's you go to in the world
they know exactly how to make a Big Mac.
A Big Mac will be made in the exact same
process and more or less taste the same
no matter where you have it in the
world. One of the biggest things that I
realized few years into my business is
that I am a McDonald's. We must document
exactly the way something is meant to be
done. We must create a standing
operating procedure. So let's say you're
a design agency today, there has to be a
way that is dictated to be like this is
how we design, this is what good looks
like and this is then how we communicate
with the client. Similarly for this
exact YouTube video there is a process.
There has been research done on how
we're going to do this video, titles and
thumbnails have been decided and
researched, then I'm sitting and
filming, then from here it's going to go
into A cut then a B cut and essentially
it'll be uploaded and put out. That is
what you call a process, a systematized
broken down process just like
McDonald's. Where you won't be able to
scale is that every time you go to do
something you're coming up with it in
that moment. And look, if you're a
slightly larger business today and you
already have processes, this is the most
important thing. You need to make sure
the person that owns the process is
actually someone with an operational
background. The biggest mistake that I
personally made and hundreds of founders
that I work with they think if you have
a guy in your company today that's the
best at doing something that means they
should be the one running that process,
owning that process. For example, if you
do have a design agency and you the best
designer in the business should not be
the one who's creating the process and
keeping it up to date. The reason is
it's a different skill. People that are
good at making processes are very
operationally driven and they can break
down things into systematic small
points. People are normally good at
something in my experience they're
really good at doing the thing but
they're terrible at managing the
operations to do with it. So in my
experience what I did was I brought in
people who had operational background
and they set up the processes, the SOPs
like McDonald's. So at Gymreapers today
if we're going to do something we have
the playbook of how are we going to do
it. That is kept up to date. There's V1,
V2, V3. We need to make sure we keep
evolving this process and keep adding to
it and get everybody to add to it. So it
becomes the way of doing something. So
someone cannot whilst they're making
their burger decide to pour two spoons
of ketchup if it's only one spoon. And
that is where you'll be able to control
the quality in your business and
actually be able to have a system in
place to make sure things happen to a
certain standard that you are happy
with. Because yes, as you scale, as
you're busy, you cannot be worrying
about is the work going to get done to
the highest quality possible that you
want as the founder CEO. The other
problem related to that is that when you
hire new people as you scale, someone's
going to come in and they're going to be
like no, no, no, I know what to do, I
have experience, I'm just going to do it
my way. And the answer is no. So at
Gymreapers today we use Asana for task
tracking. Every day when someone new
joins us I want to use Notion, the
answer is no. Not good enough. You have
to follow the way. Why? Because we're
McDonald's. We're going to make the
stuff the way it's meant to be made and
that is it. And that is how you lead
from the front when it comes to
systematizing the business. The second
one that you must install in your
business is a system for growth which is
all about OKRs, objectives and key
results. In order for everybody to be
moving towards the same goal as a
business, the same North Star, it has to
be obvious to everybody in the business
that that is what it is. So if the goal
is a certain revenue number, certain
amount of clients, certain amount of
products sold, certain amount of social
media followers, it has to be known. So
on this channel my objective is to
become an authority online in the
business space. The key result to
measure that would be a hundred thousand
subscribers. So that is an objective
with the key result. Then you would
break that down further and give it to
different people. So the objective for
someone who's editing videos would be to
make sure the retention is 30% plus so
people don't drop off. So the point is
that even without you having to be there
physically and remind people, people
already know what is the objective of
their role, what is the objective of the
business and it has to be transparent.
You can use any OKR tool on the market
to do this exercise with your team. If
you want to know more about OKRs I would
recommend a book called Measure What
Matters by John Doerr. Essentially he
invented this philosophy and it's how he
scaled Google and many other businesses.
It's one of the greatest systems to
install in your business for growth. The
third system we need to put in place is
all about numbers and metrics. Look, the
truth is people lie and numbers don't.
So this is very, very critical for the
success of your business and for you to
build a business which will have equity
value one day. In the world of AI and in
the world of automations it is very easy
to automate reports. The downside of
that is that people don't actually look
at those numbers. So what I'm a big
believer of is creating a manual
dashboard of KPIs that you are looking
at for your business which has to be
calculated and updated every single day.
So I'll give you an example. For one of
my businesses Neurotonic which is behind
me, which is this amazing brand all
about helping your brain work better,
and we have a daily dashboard. And
ultimately every single day someone has
to manually pull all the data which
basically means the following. They need
to go and look at how much traffic came,
how much we sold, what products actually
sold, what were the conversion rates,
what were the affiliate sales, what was
the product mix, how many customer
service tickets, how many returns, how
much this, how much that. A lot of that
data could get pulled automatically for
sure but somebody has to look at that
data, they have to create a report with
some insight and then they share that
report in Slack 12 p.m. every single
day, 365 days a year. Yes, this clarity
allows me and my team and the whole
Neurotonic team in a second to know
exactly how the business is performing.
So I may be busy, I have many businesses
now, I have a team of 150 people but all
I got to do is click on that Slack
channel and in two seconds I can see
exactly how the business is performing
and I can see if it what the percentages
are, is it increasing week on week, what
are the metrics, is it going down and I
can step in if I need to. That's also
applicable for everybody that's involved
in the business cuz one of the biggest
reasons you cannot build a business
which scaled is lack of data. They
actually don't know what's going on and
people conceal information instead of
building a system which allows everybody
to have centralized information, daily
information that they can move forward
with. I know what you're thinking, hey
Sean, why would I do that? If you need
the data just go and get it yourself,
right? Just log into Shopify and people
can just find the data. They won't and
that is the point. You got to build a
system which brings the data to people
but not just data cuz no one looks at
it. It's got to be with insight. So
someone has to look at it and be like
yesterday was a bad day and here's the
problems that I can see because people
will tell you things are going great.
You look at the numbers and they're like
well, the conversion rate is down 2% on
mobile. Let's do something about it,
right? So very, very important, get your
numbers together, build a system so your
business can scale. The last system is
all about the founder system.
Essentially what is your role when it
comes to scaling your business today? I
like to follow a rule which is the 10 80
10 rule. And the way it works is every
single major thing in your business you
need to do the first 10%. Then you're
going to get your team to do the 80%.
And in the final 10% you're going to
step in to make sure it's done
correctly. And that is the system that
has allowed me to scale to a hundred
million dollars plus. The reason is the
following. So in the first 10% what are
we focusing on? You want to be the one
that sets the direction of the thing
we're about to do. So for example, I'm
currently working on a new webinar, it's
called the monetization summit. So I'm
going to work on the first 10%. So that
means yes, I'm actually going to do some
work myself. I have to sit and think
about the angle, the messaging, the
price, the way we're going to structure
it, who's going to do what. Then I'm
going to hold the first one, two, three,
four, five meetings myself. I'm going to
clarify the objectives of this project.
I'm going to make it very clear who's
responsible for what and I'm going to
make sure it has a clear SOP and it's
gone into the task management system and
it's very, very clear. And then I'm
going to step away and I'm going to let
the team do their magic because they're
amazing. They have to do that 80% of
work. And this is where you don't want
to micromanage people. You got to let
people bring their own genius out. You
got to let people go ahead and do what
they want to do. So they have free roam
to go and execute that the way they see
best cuz we want people to be
innovative. We want people to have
ideas. And then the final 10% is when I
come back. Ultimately I knew exactly
what I set out to do and now the team
has done the work. My job is to review
that, give any feedback and then it's
ready to go. It can go out the door.
Meaning we're going going live with the
project, we're going to push it live.
We're going to do whatever. But 10 80 10
is the way. You cannot step away and
think your team is just going to do
stuff. That's where things start to
break because they won't know what they
don't know. And you're the founder for a
reason because you have that instinctive
thinking on a lot of stuff that others
in your team will not. And the business
is only where it is today because of
that skill that you have. So, you don't
want to let that go. To today, even with
the 150 people, even with the portfolio
of businesses, I'm very involved in that
10 80 10 way. And then I'm making sure
we're doing things in the way I see
because of what I know. Team does their
part, and then it's back to me to review
it. And it's a great framework for
anybody. So, look, with that being said,
those are my four systems that you must
install today in order to build a
scalable business. My name is Shawn. I
make two to three videos every single
week on this channel. Make sure you hit
subscribe, and I'll see you in the next
video.

to action? And what's going to happen
there? Then we know when that call is
booked, when you're making our deck, we
are now also thinking about that
message. And that message is going to
continue cuz that deck should be saying
the same message. And that's exactly how
you sign clients. Because you've honed
in on the problem that you know people
have. And the whole journey, your sales
journey is fixated on solving this
problem. and why wouldn't the client
sign up with you? And that is it, man.
You want to build a big agency, you want
to make money, you want to buy the Roly,
you want to be on the yacht, you want to
be famous, that's the you're going
to have to do to make all that happen.
You want to buy your mom and dad a
house, you want to fly them around the
world, you want to propose to your
girlfriend, whatever those things are is
how we're going to do it. That being

what's hard and we need to grow and this

because you're not thinking long-term to

goals and thinking will completely
change the way you operate as a person

yeah, look, that's all I really wanted
to cover in this video. It's like that's
the stuff that like frustrates me,
excites me, pushes me, and what I'm like

put that out there, you know, and I'll
summarize it one more time cuz I think
it's extremely extremely important. You
have to be evolving as a person. That's
just who you need to become if you are
not today. You got to stop almost
mentally like sabotaging yourself
because you can't think you're getting
like I'm overwhelmed. I can't do this. I
can't do that. It's too much to do.
You're not pulling the trigger. You got
to pull the trigger fast. Make a
decision. You're doing it. Let's do it.
Well, you know what? My biggest thing is
when I sit down with my clients, I'll be
like, "This is the course, this is the
name, this is the price, this is the
this, let's do it." And they've been
there for 3 months thinking, "What's the
right price?" Nah, bro said this in the
video. Oh, this girl said this. I

This is it. Let's go. And look, you are
going to get haters. It doesn't matter.
You have to keep working on your skills,
as I said. And ultimately, you got to
play for the long-term game. If you do
all that stuff in that way, you will

## 52. How I raised $3.7M for Neutonic with Chris Williamson and James Smith

So, we have just raised $3.7 million at
a 20 million valuation for our brand
Newtonic. In this video, I'm going to be
showing you exactly how we did it, who
invested, and I'm going to be walking
you through the exact pitch deck that I
used to raise this money. But first
things first, if you don't know what
Newtonic is, Newtonic is a brand which
is going to help your brain work better.
Ultimately myself, I am the co-founder
of the business alongside Chris
Williamson who you may know of who has
one of the biggest podcasts in the world
with modern wisdom, James Smith who is a
massive creator and an author and sells
out arenas around the world and the
manager Luke who likes to remain
anonymous on the internet. So firstly,
how is this business set up? And this
information is public anyway. So I own
20% of Newtonic. James Smith, he owns
37%, Chris owns 23% and Luke also owns
20%. And what's crazy, I guess, about
this raise that we've done, we first
raised um $2 million from an
institutional investor called JDI
Investments who are amazing, super high
growth guys. And then we brought in an
amazing group of entrepreneurs and
creators to essentially build a group of
people that can help us take this brand
all the way to $100 million plus. We
have Kayla, one of the biggest fitness
creators in the world. Alan Barrett,
who's the founder of Grenade. Everybody
here has probably had a Grenade protein
bar. He sold that for 200 million. He's
come on board. We have Dan Martell who
has had successful businesses in the
past and has a big audience. We have
Cody Sanchez who's been a massive fan of
the brand the moment we started.
Obviously he's a massive creator now and
a business expert. We have Mike
Thirsten, we have June, we have Wendy,
we have Lara Costa, we have Chris
Donnelly, Darren Paula Lima, we have
Seth who's big in the BJ space. We got
Daniel Priestley. We have an amazing
group of investors who have all joined
Etonic and I'm so excited to be working
with them and ultimately having people
support the brand and together we're
going to build one of the biggest brands
in the world and we're already on that
track. Some other quick facts here. We
launched the business in November 2023
and since then we've done $9 million in
sales and a lot of the time we were out
of stock because we funded the whole
thing ourself. So it was tough to keep
everything in stock while we're selling
in the UK and US at the same time. Also
we did launch this brand in the UK and
US simultaneously on our website and on
Amazon and also then in the UK we
launched it on Tik Tok shop. So we were
multi channel from day one. It was
extremely tough to obviously keep every
single channel in stock but still we've
had a great start and in that time we
not only got to $9 million we also
stayed profitable as a business which is
unheard of for any new startup
especially in the CBG space where you
actually have a consumable product. Not
only that, since then we launched two
other products. We launched a focus
blend which is a powder. I believe I had
one here and then we also launched some
capsules as well just creating different
formats to have the product. So with
that being said, let me quickly take you
back to how it all came together. So I
was personally very interested in the
neutropic space had seen it as almost
like the white space and and also like
the productivity space meaning that
people want to be more efficient and
people want to be more switched on,
people want to get more done. Everybody
like everybody is trying to live their
life in the best way possible but there
in the market products don't really do
that and like most of us you know for my
whole life I've taken caffeine right you
have caffeine to give you that boost to
get things done when you look at the
science there's so many other things and
that's what you call neutropic
ingredients like other neutropics that
can help your brain function at a higher
level I was interested in this space and
I saw that James Smith who was a creator
he was also creating content on this

know uh Luke his manager. So we ended up

brand and sell product to help solve

you know what let's do some initial

## 61. How to Get Clients to Pay You More (Works Every Time)

Okay, so in this video I'm going to be
telling you about how to increase the
lifetime value of your clients, which
basically means that on day one, if
you're going to sell something to a
client for $2,000, how are we going to
turn that into $10,000 with the same
clients? The truth is, I've been doing
the exact same thing to scale my agency,
Gemflow, I would sign a client on 2,
three, four, $5,000, and the same client
today, I am making anywhere between $30
to $50,000 from. And there's a certain
way to do that. But in this video, I'm
not only going to be telling you how to
do that. that I'm actually going to be
ranking all the different ways that
exist for you to increase the lifetime
value so you can focus on the right
stuff to scale your service-based
business, your agency, etc. With that
being said, let's jump on my screen and
I have the classic S tier grid right
here. I've written out all the different
things that I do. I've seen people talk
about and I'm going to go through them
one by one and tell you what is the best
ones for you to do. Not only that, I'll
actually be walking you through some of
the stuff on what should you actually do
to implement some of this stuff. So,
this is not just about I'm going to rank
them. I'm also going to give you some
detail below. And look at anytime if
you're like, "Sean, I love what you're
talking about and I want to get more
money out of my existing clients. I want
to grow. I want to scale." All you need
to do is click the link down below, book
a call with myself or my team, and we
can help you do just that. With that
being said, let's jump straight in.
Ultimately, why do you want to do this,
right? We want to keep our clients for
the longest time. We want them to get
them to pay us more over time and you we
want to grow with them. So, just before
we dive in, you know, the the secret to
building a big agency, you know, I've
done $100 million in revenue is the
lifetime value of your clients. It's not
the more clients you bring in. Nobody
wants to have an agency with 300 clients
that you're managing. That sounds like
hell. What you want in is you want high
ticket clients. You want the best
clients in the world and you want to
keep them for a very long time. And this
is the stuff that you'll have to be
doing. and you'll see what stuff is the
best stuff to do to achieve that. I'm
going to pick as the first one is
providing a great service. So obviously
as a service providers, our job is to

where do I rank providing you know a
service which is good in terms of
keeping your clients. If I'm being
completely honest, I would say that
actually is not that important. It's
important but not that important. One of
the biggest things I realized early on
when someone signs up with you and your
job is to provide the service, if that's
all you're going to do, you will
actually lose that client at some point.
One of the things that your job is that
every time you are speaking to your
client, your job is to instill impress
them. Your job is to make them think
that you're such an expert that you're
thinking about the next thing. If you
think your job is to get on the call,
give them an update on how the ads are
performing, and that's it, you are
wrong. You should be saying, "Here's two
other things we want to launch. I a new
offer, more creatives, more ideas, you
should always be on the front foot. So
just providing a great service, I would
say it's like, you know, it's it's it's
B- level in terms of increasing lifetime
value. Cuz essentially, to be honest,
I'm actually going to move it to C
because you're not going to increase the
lifetime value. This is what you're
doing to get paid. And that's all that's
going to happen. At some point, they
will think they can do it cheaper,
better, in-house because there's nothing
more that you're doing for them by just
doing the service. When I say this to
agency owners, they're like, "What? I
just thought the whole game is bring
clients in, service them, and just do
that again and again, right? And that
that's how you build an agency. And the
truth is that's not how you increase the
lifetime value of your clients because
that's what they're paid for. And if
you're not on the front foot, it's not

that every time we're going to have a

have something in our back pocket. And
what your job should be as the founder,

you've got to try to meet your client
one- on-one. if that's once a month,
every few months to try to really
impress them with the next best thing to
do. That is the secret. So, the how I've

high ticket clients. My name is Sean,

want to have clients that pay us small
amounts of money, $300, $400, $2,000 per
month. You want to have clients which
pay us $5, $10,000 plus per month. And
you want to get percentage of people's

## 67. How to create a $1M Course [step by step guide]

Selling digital products, i.e. courses,
is one of the biggest opportunities in
the world today. It's super easy to do,
highly scalable, and insanely
profitable. So, in this video, I'm going
to show you exactly how you can make a
course where can sell more than a
million dollars plus. My name is Sean.
I'm the founder of gemflow.com, an
agency that behind the scenes helps the
biggest creators in the world sell
digital products. I have sold $300
million of digital products alone and in
that $und00 million on courses in
itself. And in this video, I'm going to
show you exactly what you should and
shouldn't be doing when creating your
first product and how do you actually
make a course that can sell that much.
But before we dive into that, there's
something we need to talk about. 50%
people think courses are amazing and
they want to learn and 50% people think
they are scams. So you're probably
watching this video thinking I don't
want to sell a course because I don't
want to be like that and courses are not
real, etc. So I thought what's best to

clients to bring him. But he's so

the next cash collected in, right? Make

per month just from me, but he missed it

owners. And I loved it, right? Because I
have, you know, so many of my
competitors have gone bust because they
didn't think like that. They didn't
think long term. It's crazy. You know,
some of my clients have been with for
seven plus years, which is insane to
think. I've literally like grown up with
my clients in that time. I've got
married, had kids, and I still got the
same clients. Um, which is which is mad.
And look, that is the complete full
service stack model that I used. Right?
So build skills, email people,
communicate with people and tell them
what you can actually do for them, then
focus on
delivering that, then immediately
solving the next problem for them to

## 75. How to work super hard without missing out on life

I've worked 12 hours a day for the past
5 years and I still take my kids to
school every day. I put them to sleep. I
still spend time with my wife and I get
to enjoy the money that I make. And the
truth is, my companies have done more
than $150 million
in revenue without burning out and
without sacrificing the most important
things in life, which is your family and
the people that you love. So, the truth
is, I figured out how to perform at the
highest level and not giving up the
things that you really care about in
life. In today's video, I'm going to
walk you through how I manage my time,
have certain times for like really
peak performance work, have
other times to spend with my family, and
then have other time to manage the whole
day and everything else. The truth is,
it is my unique way of performing at the
highest self for a number of years and
still making sure I absolutely love what
I do. The very first thing is that
working for 12 hours straight is not
physically possible and is not going to
give you the best results. And all of us
are different. We all have times in the
day where we work the best. And some
people like to get up at 4:00 a.m. and
do that, but for me personally, it's
always been the case of the first 3
hours of me sitting down to work are the
best moment for me to be able to do
things that are going to help the
business grow, the most important tasks,
and the final 3 hours of the day is
where I also work the best. It's this
interesting mix of start the day, get on
the most important tasks, then as the
day goes on, at the end of the day, you
now know what you did and didn't do in
the day, so you can then once again
focus on the most important things at
the end of the day. I've always been a
night person, so for me, I've engineered
my day and my productivity and my family
life all to fit that really well. So, I
start my day at 10:00 a.m. and I'll be
up till 2:00 a.m. And the rest of this
video, I'm going to show you the full
breakdown of my day, but just so you
have context of you got to do what works
for you. Even when I was younger, when I
was doing my accounting exams, I would
be the person who's late at night in the
library studying or trying to study at
home, mainly because I prefer to be
doing things at night. So, you got to
find your own peak performance time and
lean into that. The second most
important thing is allocating the tasks
to the right time period in the day. So,
for me personally, what does that mean
is that I want to start my day
essentially doing all the tasks that are
expected of me. As a CEO of a business
with 150 plus staff and a port folio of
businesses now with another 50 staff, it
is hard. So, I like to make sure at my
first 3 hours, I am unblocking everybody
else. Because I don't want it to be that
anyone else is waiting for me so things
can move forwards. I start my day with
this mental thinking that let me unblock
everybody. Let me make sure everyone
else is productive so we can together be
productive as a business. The other side
advantage is that I'm based in Dubai,
which means I'm essentially ahead in
time. So, even though I start my day at
10:00, it's only 6:00 a.m. in the UK.
So, everybody's asleep. And my US team
are also just finishing their day. It's
10:00 p.m. in in LA and Eastern time
zone is 1:00 a.m. and they're already
asleep. So, it's a unique time for me to
start my day. In a way, I'm actually
earlier than my team and my job is to
unblock everybody so nobody's waiting
for me. When they start the day, they
can go off and start doing stuff. So,
figuring out what to do when is very
important when it comes to maximizing
your own efficiency and what you're
doing in your 12 hours a day like I do.
And then from that, the middle of the
day, I by design would spend 3:00 till
6:00
dedicating to calls, meetings, client
calls, etc. So, 3:00 till 6:00 p.m. will
be fully focused on things that I'm not
physically doing myself but I have to
partake in. I think for me, what you
can't do is if you want to work and
perform the best, you cannot be
switching between stuff. You cannot be
like doing a call, then try and do some
work, then try and do a call, then you
got to like fix some problem, and you're
jumping around. Structuring your day in
the way you see fit is how I'm able to
work at such a high level, do so many
things at once, and essentially enjoy it
because I'm doing it under my terms. One
of the biggest secrets to productivity
is just doing things in your own terms
versus having to do things according to
someone else's schedule, way of life,
plan, etc. And that's essentially what
leads to someone burning out, not
feeling motivated, not feeling like
doing something cuz you're not doing the
way you want it to do it. I'm filming
this video right now at 1:30 a.m. in the
morning, and you'll see that later in
this video. Essentially, this is peak
time when I'm working on growth, and
that is why this is an important time
frame for me to film content, right? But
it works for me. Everything has been
taken care of today. I unblocked the
team, had a bunch of meetings, done lots
of stuff in the day, that at this time
it's the me time, it's focused on
growth. The third thing for me is that
you have to have pattern interrupts in
your day. Essentially, what that means
is something which is going to refresh
you, and it's going to stop you in in in
like the deep work, in the stress, in
the work stuff. So, for me, going back
to it, that would be between 2:00 to
3:00 p.m. every day, I go and pick my
daughter up from school. So, for me, I
literally have to leave the my desk, I
have to go, I go pick her up. It doesn't
take too long, I'll play some games with
her, we come back, I eat some food, she
eats some food, a bit of family time,
and that 2:00 till 3:00 essentially
completely resets the day mentally
because I've already done 3 hours of
deep work. And by that time, after that
1 hour, I'm now ready to go again. So,
for you, what does that mean? Find
things that will take you away from the
work, but then it makes you want to come
back to it 10 times stronger and more
focused. What I tend to avoid is just
sitting and watching Netflix cuz it's
not necessarily a break per se. I like
to stop thinking about the thing I'm
thinking about, so when I come back to
it, I'll be fresher to think about it
then. It's very important to have some
sort of interruption in your day, which
feels like an interruption, and actually
works in a weird way to re-motivate you,
to get you going again. So, you almost
feel like you're starting the day again.
It's a massive hack for me, and also it
makes me subconsciously happy. I got to
see my family. I got to focus on other
few family things, and that gives you
back in the zone to be like, "Okay, now
I'm ready to go again." That actually
brings you to a very important point,
which is you've also got to curate your
environment. To be productive, to be
able to work at a very high level,
you've got to create a workspace for
you. This is my home office, essentially
which is also the studio. This is my
workspace. There's one bedroom in a
house which I've dedicated to my office.
If I'm in this room, I'm working, and I
only work in this room. I don't sit
downstairs on my laptop. I'm not lying
in bed or trying to do some work. I sit
in this room when it's time for work,
and that's all I do in this room, and
that makes a massive difference. Cuz the
moment I walk through those doors, I
know what I'm meant to do in this room,
and that is what I do. I think a lot of
people think, "Oh, I'm going to go to a
coffee shop for a day work, etc." That's
never been me. For me, my environment
and the way I work is the most important
thing. I've got multiple screens. Got my
full setup here. I'm like in the command
center, ready to go to get done,
and I need to be in my own space. Also,
I tend to work the best alone. I can't
even have anyone else in the room. We
used to have an office years ago, and it
was very different, but I remember I
would sit in the office all day, and
when I would come home, I'd finally feel
like I could do some work. So, since
COVID and since we went to hybrid and we
have offices, I just love being in my
own space, and I just love to I've
curated the best setup for myself, and I
advise anybody that's struggling to have
the productivity, and someone who's not
being able to create that zone of genius
for themselves where they can really
operate, I think you have to change your
environment and make it what you like.
You know, everything in this room is
done to exactly my taste, my style, how
I want things. So, I actually love
sitting in here and it's a dream come
true. Create that space for yourself and
you'll see that no longer it'll feel
like work cuz you're kind of like
enjoying everything. Not even the work
itself, you're enjoying where you're
sitting, what you're doing, and the
whole feeling of that. It makes a
massive difference. The next big unlock
is that you have to exercise. So, for
me, going back to the start of the day,
10:00 a.m. to 11:00 a.m., I actually
start every single day by exercising.
First, for me, it just wakes me up
because the moment I exercise, you start
sweating, it just gets you fired up, and
your whole body wakes up, and you are
ready to conquer the day. Tomorrow as
I'll probably exercise every single day,
either it's weights, or it's running, or
it's cycling, or I run outside. No
matter what it is, I just love going
through that. I love listening to music.
So, you know, music is on, doing some
workout, and you just get ready for the
day. Again, that 1 hour sets me up for
the first work block, which was then
11:00 till 2:00, and it's a perfect way.
Going back to pattern interruption,
right? You'll see how my day is
structured. I'm starting off with an
hour of gym, 3 hours of work, hour of
family time, and that brings you to the
next block, which is 3:00 p.m. to 6:00
p.m., and that is the time I'm doing the
calls, the meetings, the client calls,
whatever, and trying to squeeze as much
I possibly can in those 3 hours. The
very next point is that you have to have
the balance. You know, I'm not a
believer of like work-life balance and
how people describe that in the market.
For me, it's about you got to balance
everything in your life according to
your own needs and wants and desires.
And for me, that is if I'm not working,
the only thing I want to do is spend
time with my family. Like that's that's
pretty much it. If as long as I got to
do those two things in a day, it's a
successful day. And I managed to do that
while working 12 hours a day, so I'm
super happy. So, that brings me to the
time between 6:00 p.m. and 8:00 p.m. is
when I get to do that. That is me
playing with my kids, having dinner,
putting my kids to sleep, chilling with
my wife. In those 2 hours, I'll wrap all
of that stuff in, and it'll be
absolutely amazing. And that again just
fulfills me internally, gives me what I
need, and then I'm back into work mode.
So even for you, that balance that
you're chasing for, if that's for you to
sit alone and read a book, cool. If
that's for you to go a walk, cool. If
that's for you to go and see your
friends, cool. Whatever that thing is,
put it in your calendar. It's a
dedicated time space. You don't book
meetings into that and you ensure that
happens. But from there, essentially, we
have to move into what I call the prime
time. And for me, from 8:00 p.m. to
11:00 p.m., I'm back into a 3-hour block
where I'm doing calls and meetings. And
the reason I have to have it at that
time is because of the US businesses
that I manage and everybody else. I
essentially then I have to get on those
calls, manage all that side of the
business. And then from 11:00 p.m. to
2:00 a.m., I'm back into my genius
hours, the hours where I'm sitting and
thinking about growth. And I'm forcing
myself not to do operational stuff, not
to answer people's questions, not to
unblock other people. We're going to do
that in the morning. Not to do client
calls. I'm predominantly working on
growth. Most founders today just don't
dedicate enough time to think of the
next five moves. You know, me creating
content is part of that because I need
to build a personal brand. That is why
I'm filming this and now it's almost,
you know, it's 1:45 a.m. I'm in my work
block. As soon as I finish with this
video, I'm done. Then I'm going to chill
for a bit. I'm going to go to sleep. So
normally, 11:00 till 2:00 a.m., I'll be
working on growth stuff, the stuff that
only I can work on. I need to be alone.
I love that it's dark. I love that it's
quiet. I love that my kids and family,
everyone is asleep. I'm up. I'm work
strategizing. I'm going to get
done. And then 2:00 till 2:30, to be
honest, I'll probably eat something.
It's a bad habit. And I'll probably
watch something for a little bit and
then essentially like be tired enough to
go to sleep. I sleep less during the
week cuz on the weekend I recover. But
that's just me. Look, and that is it.
That is how I work 12 hours a day. If
you add all those times together, that
comes to 12 hours a day. And I've been
doing this for a very long time. Very,
very long time. And I absolutely love it
because what I've managed to achieve,
starting from zero to doing uh
$25 million in revenue for my agency and
another $20 million plus for my
portfolio of businesses in a single
year, it's because of my productivity.
It's because of the way I've scheduled
and set up my day. I honestly believe
everyone can work more efficiently if
they just structure the whole time
around it. Where most people go wrong,
and I guess the lesson is forget 9-5 or
9-6. You don't need to work on the hours
the world has said are working hours.
You should do it in the way you want to
do it because you'll be able to perform
at the highest level ever.

to be a creator today. It's very

to come and buy something. I'm saying

And that is why a lot of your businesses

service to people and people are signing

the thing that you're trying to do for

yes reaches a lot of people. He has a

more of whatever you're trying to sell.
And the fourth thing was we started

to just get more audience, get more
traffic, get more leads, get more

free events, organic content, sending
emails, paid ads are the four ways that
essentially we took that idea and went
to market and made a success. Ultimately

so. And the key thing in all that is
that wherever you send your traffic to,
you have to make sure your landing page
is really optimized. We did something on
credit college page is that we really
honed in on what do people actually get?
Because a lot of people want to know
tangibly if I was to join or pay etc.
What will I actually get? So when we

consistently worked on optimizing our
page. A lot of business owners are like

constantly be looking at it, looking at

we've been doing. And that's why each
essential intake of people coming in,
new customers, new clients, you know,
we've managed to get that conversion
rate higher and higher because that is
the right way to be thinking about it.
That brings me on to number three, that
you actually have to do a really good
job. So to increase retention, to
increase reviews, to increase people

have done from the very beginning, I

staying on calls for longer. I'm talking

question. Me and the team are spending

sure every single thing that we promised
them we are overd delivering on. Why?
The truth is we don't need to. The truth
is we're probably doing it too much. The
truth is we could chase growth and do

work, you it'll pay you dividends for

agency, Genflow. I went overboard for my

and people remember essentially when you
were there for them and you did things

unnoticed. Same you can say in life with

You'll always remember those things. So
it's very critical at this time for us
to be doing that by design and it's
helped grow the business. And then we
can see this in our reviews. If you go
to a YouTube channel, there's reviews
there. On the website, there's reviews
and there's reviews are now driving new

and this whole thing is going to work.
And that's a massive, massive plus
point. So, whatever your thing is, it
doesn't end from making the sale. It
ends when the person is a happy
customer. So, you got to keep doing what
you're doing to make that work. So,
look, if you have a new business, if
you're looking to start a business,
follow that framework. Find a big enough
opportunity. Make sure you've really
dialed in the different ways you're
going to get the attention of people.
And lastly, make sure you have a solid
product so people will keep coming back

## 82. I lost a client. This sucks.

Yes, just lost a client and the crazy
thing is so just lost this client
essentially that I've been helping make
millions of dollars for the past like
four years. I took them from like a few
hundred,000 to basically like 1.5
million, I think 2 million and then like
3 million and um it's never really
happened to me before and you can
probably see in the way I'm speaking and
my tone and just everything is like it
it's by surprise. The crazy thing about
it is that I only met them like two
weeks ago and we sat and went through
like the full plan and what we're doing
and basically all the stuff we normally
do. And it turns out that basically they
turned up to that meeting just pretended
to just like nod along and be like,
"Yeah, yeah, yeah." And all that time
essentially they were planning on giving
notice. Not only that, they essentially
have like gone and done something else
and um and it was just like fake
basically. And you know, it feels so
weird when you've like worked with
somebody for like 4 years and for them
to essentially just be completely fake
and pretend to still be like the same,
you know, and essentially we were
working together for a very long time,
making a lot of money together, help
them become millionaires, cash
millionaires this is. So yeah, we had
this meeting, everything is fine. And
then they basically say um you know um
essentially they send an email with a
notice and just talking about surprise
like no warning, no message, no nothing,
no conversation, just straight you know
boom and
um I know it's business and everyone
says that I say it too. It's not
personal and all that stuff but you just
think there'll be like common decency or
the way people do business the way
people are. But I think the truth is
that people don't care. People only care
about themselves. And I get it, right?
Like I don't like have any bad like
thing about it. It's just that when
you've put in so much work and the one
thing in the agency space is that when
you do work for other people, you
obviously do a lot of work for people to
make them make them successful,
especially in the thing that I do for
people cuz I literally do everything for
people. um for it to then happen in this
way. Yeah. It is not it doesn't doesn't
feel good. The crazy thing is that so
after they gave notice, I got on a call
with them and I was still thinking, you
know what, I just need to get on a call
with them and like talk to them about
this, figure out what's going on. In
short, they had someone else approach
them. Basically, that sold to them that
they can take them from where they are
now and they can make them even bigger.
Like they can like 4x their business and
they're going to kill it. And what's
crazy to me is that they essentially
basically said yes, they've signed a
deal with this other
person and like 4 years of
relationship/work gone in a second just
because someone else came along and said
I can do it better which is just crazy
that you think you'll build some
goodwill over this time and not just
think whatever and jump ship.
Um so yeah anyway I got I get on this
call to and I'm thinking you know I'm
going to smooth things over. I'm good at
this and you know it is one of my skills
to like literally talk people through
stuff and get people to see the logic
and get them to like you know make the
right decision and um I get on the call.
So the clients
themselves didn't even turn the camera
on like four years of working together
and they couldn't even bother to even
turn the camera on. They've added some
other guy on the call. He introduces
himself that I'm now their advisor. I'm
just like what? Like I'm literally
talking to the client saying, "Yo, what
what what's going on? It's been 4 years.
We made you guys like this much last
year. We have a clear plan. Look at the
numbers. Shit's going so well, but you
guys are like saying you want to leave
to do what?" And I was like, "So this
other party that has now said you're
going to be this much bigger. How like
what's the plan? Like why do you think
what have they said?" Obviously, I'm
like slightly surprised and upset and
stuff, but what I was more shocked about
is that they couldn't they didn't even
turn the cameras on and they have this
some other guy. I'm like, "Who are you?
Why are you speaking?" Um, I'm trying to
speak to these guys that are basically
my clients and just, you know, um, and
it just shows, man, like it's just what
it what the world is like and what it is
going to be like. And I'm grown up
enough and been doing this for long
enough to know that this happens
and it's business. And I kind of wanted
to make this video because no matter how
successful you are, how big you are, you
may see me and the clients that I work
with and our success, you know, $23
million last year, whatever, but you
still have to deal with this sort of
stuff. And this is the real life stuff,
which is tough. Um, and one thing I have
to tell myself as well, I can't let it
get to me. You can probably tell in this
video though, it is getting to me, but
it's more I think on the principle, more
not so like the loss of revenue or
anything else. This is more like from a
personal principle point of view. If you
want to leave, that's fine. Just do it
in the right way. You know, give tell
people why and do it in the right way
and whatever. You have the right to
leave and do whatever. It's going to
happen, right? Like in any business, in
anything, there's always going to be a a
life cycle almost, right? Someone's
going to join, someone's going to leave
a certain time. It's going to happen. I
have it's happened many times before.
It's not like I've not lost a client
historically. It's just this just the
way it happened. I wanted to kind of
share because it may happen to you as
well and you always need to I would say
you should think that 20% of your
clients are going to snake you basically
in some way or the other. They may be
badmouthing you in the market but still
paying you. That's happened to me. You
know, they may quit the way I've just
explained the story. Um and that may
happen
to and you know, you just need to
embrace it. like there's going to be 20%
of your clients, your customers or
whoever it is that going to do something
that you never expect and you're going
to have to learn how to deal with it and
you're going to have to learn, that's
why I said the word embrace it because I
know it will happen. If you're going to
get to any level of success in your
life, it is going to happen. Um, but
yeah, we lose some money, you know,
that's the end of it. And if I was to be
real about it, a client is just a
client. They are just numbers. Now, my
job is to replace those numbers with
some new numbers. And I'm blessed enough
to be in a business where we're thriving
that I can go and do that. But yeah,
that being
said, hopefully no more clients losing
anytime soon. I'll see you in the next
video.

be less than 5 million in revenue. You

up to 30 million plus. And they're

like a five times return of their money.

Stripe, the next whatever in when
they're investing. How venture capital

they raise money from a bunch of people

earlier in your journey. So the reason
if you've ever done outreach or you're

of that investor and the stage that
they're invested in. Investment thesis

understands this when it comes to
looking for the right investor. So
you'll see that this investor put 100
million to this business and you're

understand, go on the website, look at

in. So if you do have a AI startup,

stage tech investors, VCs, and they

stage VCs are looking for fast growth.
Growth stage VCs are looking for slower

that's essentially how if you go and

already done hundreds of millions in

Essentially again, they will have

Private equity are happy with a less

equity are the guys that will come and

to have an investment bank like Deloitte

with you, couple of meetings and want to

stage business today, you're a founder,
that would be the best way cuz you're

believes in your vision and wants to now
help you and make it a reality and make
it worth hundreds of millions of dollars
in years to come. And the very last is
what you call trade. This is where
you're not raising money from investors,
you're actually raising money from other
businesses that are essentially bigger
than you and want to invest in your
business. Perfect example of this would
be Open AI. They raised from Microsoft,
but once again, they've raised from
trade, which essentially means other
businesses. The point with trade is it's
different, right? Like you want to
combine your resources with theirs and
you want to try to go bigger, better
together and ultimately build a bigger
business. Cuz the downside of venture
capital and private equity is that they
can only give you money and some
connections, they don't have any
infrastructure. If you raise from trade,
they have infrastructure. So,
essentially, if I invest in your
business, tomorrow you could have the
infrastructure that I have at my

were able to scale it so fast because as
Jam Flow invested in Nutanix, which is a

which I had already developed, multiple
warehouses, supply chain, etc., e-comm,
content for us to be able to scale that
much faster. And that's ultimately the
benefit for a founder to go straight to
trade. The downside is that once you
have done that, it's very hard to then
go and raise from some of the other
guys. Because when someone's coming from
a trade point of view, they're most
likely going to buy you out in full over
a 5-10 year period and most trade deals
actually would say that in the
agreement, if the business hits certain
milestones, they will have the right to
buy the whole business out. You wouldn't
be able to then say, you know what, I'm
going to go to some VC and I'm going to
raise more money on the side. It won't
work like that. So, those are the
different levels of investment. So, in
reality, you have to actually understand
where you are as a business today. You
got to look for what type of investor do
you actually want based on where you are
and then you got to go and try to get
them. The best way is to raise capital.

like I can't work with these guys
because they just literally I have to
chase them to even get a response and
I'm paying them to do this job it just
can't be related to this is you actual
the way you prepare and do client
meetings which is part of communication
essentially is the meeting Cadence like
how do you run your meetings internally
and
externally externally especially you
have to have a clear agenda agenda
should be included within each client
meeting calendar invite ahead of the
meeting so the client knows and you know
what's actually going to happen in the
meeting secondly every single call
internal external especially client
calls you should be taking meeting
minutes yes so you can then immediately
follow up with exactly what was
discussed what's the plan and what were
the actual action next steps even
yourself in the call you have to make
sure every single call you are finishing
off with okay we have agreed the
following this is the next steps and
then you send that in writing via email
because again before someone's going to
be like what am I paying for there's no
results I want to leave all that stuff
you'll be like they cannot complain
where if you're having regular calls
with them following up with regular
action points and if they're not doing
their bits it'll be very clear so again
it's not even just communication it's
the way you communicate and the way you
set up these things in your agency will
be the greatest way for you to have
success and look no one talks about this
stuff right this is not the sexy stuff
this is not the stuff that you think
about when you think about running an
agency but the truth is these smaller
operational details is actually how you
scale an agency it's not about a blue
ocean offer and some magical thing
that's going to happen and all of a
sudden you're going to be flooded with
clients and everything's going to be
amazing and you're just going to hire
some people and they're going to run
everything for you you have to sit and
think of these small details and I'm
telling you yourself you have to figure
out look what I've just given you you
should Implement in your agency
immediately but you need to think of the
small things that are needed in your
agency so let's say you have a lot of
remote workers today right you may have
might have a remote editors and you
naturally May having the problem of I
don't know when someone starts a day
finishes a day I don't know what task
they finished I'm still chasing them for
the last thing and that precisely we
were having the same issue so even your
internal communication so for me we have
remote teams they have to start the day
and say what's you have to actually send
a slack messages saying I'm logging in
here's my plan for the day and when they
finish the day they must send a report
saying this is what happened with our
design editors that we hire in different
places in the world we even have screen
recording software to so we know what
they were doing the day and how long
something takes them because naturally
someone's like it takes me four hours to
do something you're like how is that
possible because we know the time it
would take for something to happen and
obviously you have leeway for you know
new people Junior people Etc but they
still should be designated time frame of
something happening and the way you can
proof it is through screen recording and
there's lots of these remote softwares
now built for this exact reason so the
point being is being meticulous about
the detail will help you scale your
agency and look the fifth bonus step is
that as an entrepreneur as an agency
owner selling is such an art that it
cannot be scripted so for me I've signed
some of the biggest brands and clients
in the world and I have never used a
sales script I've never used a money by
guarantee I've never used a sales
methodology and I've tried to just
openly and honestly help the other
person which then makes the other person
want to work with you and that's the
secret of sales the problem with all the

not applicable to agencies so a lot of
the sales books to be honest most of the
large sales books or the Frameworks
stuff people talk about have come from

when someone was selling Salesforce or

things that you are trying to portray in

whatever it's very very different today
sales has just become this gimmick now
where people are getting on a call I

saying a question I give some answer he
asks me another question and it's a
never-ending game of circular questions
I'm like what are you saying I'll be
like how much do you charge well you
know it's not about you know before I
can answer that for you you know what is
it worth to you if someone was to join
if you were to have more clients how
will it feel like to you Sean if you

make you feel you're like what are you
talking about I'm asking a question how
much is your service cost tell me the
price and they can't do it so that's
where sales is today so right it started
off in this way today it's just become
that people get on calls they have a
script then they'll have all these
objection handling like tactics and then
they're going to at some point trying to
use some emotional blackmail of like so
you know imagine you could buy your mom
a house if you ad Z was success they're
trying to take it somewhere else
completely unethical immoral and to try
to make you bu a decision the worst I've
seen actually today agency owners doing
is they'll say stuff like so Sean I just
want to make sure are you the right
person is the decision maker on this
call today one guy asked me he goes are
the decision maker on this call today
like what you mean he's like you know
the person that authorizes payments
who's in charge of making the final
decision are they on the call today
because they want you to almost admit be
like yeah I'm the boss man I'm the man
of course I'm I'm the person in charge
CU later in the call they're trying to
be like so you know you need to make
this decision today it's your decision
don't talk to your business partner
don't talk to your wife don't think
about it today is the day for ACT
basically gimmicks I hate that stuff so
why I put this as a bonus step is you
want to grow an agency you got to stop
with all the you got to stop
with all the gimmicks all this stuff
that people are telling you you've got
to actually get good at solving problems

is you get on a call and you say to your
Prospect to your client that you're
talking to this is the problem you have
today I can help you solve this problem
I have done it before or I have seen it
happen online or this is why it I can
solve it for you and how and this is how
much it going to cost you this is my
base price and then I want a performance
incentive because I want to work
together with you and I'm not like the
other agencies I will send you we will
speak every single week we have weekly
calls with all almost every single one
of our clients for this exact reason and
I will make sure I respond to you I will
make sure I'll do this and I'll really
work with you and actually give a
that's it and that will help you sign
the biggest clients in the world versus
this amazing script or some objection
tactic or this
guarantee again never offered a
guarantee in the history of the company
we almost in 2025 we'll hear $100
million in the agc's revenue cuz you
don't need to anyone that is good at
providing and talking about what they're
doing they don't need to be saying well
if I can't do it for you then I'll also

salesman tactic and any good businesses
will see through it look those tactics
work on
teenagers upand cominging you know
entrepreneurs or whatever because it's

agency has done $25 million.

every single year since I started. As

have grown to $25 million this year. And
look, in this video, I'm going to be
breaking down exactly how we do that.
that. So, I'm going to break down the

something you can learn from, and also

the strategy of my business and what are

be able to apply to your business. And
look, the truth is the only reason I'm
making this video is cuz my numbers are

the UK. We are audited by PKF, one of

world. So everything I'm showing you is

these accounts. And all my previous

$25 million is what we made this year.

now I'm investing into businesses. And

and how have we managed to do that? So,

um and that has been one of the reasons

called gem flow limited which
essentially holds all the UK stuff that

as the subsidiary of the parent company

business out in the UAE and where I'm
now based myself and then we have gem

company for that
and then we have another company which I

the second thing is team cuz the truth

the average team members age in 2024 was
31 years the overall retention at the
company is about 75% and that's normally

like remote people that we hire and

which is you want to increase the

people are staying with you and we manag
to do that how we managed to do that was
I wanted to share this like one key
thing that I realized that when you're
hiring people you have to be the most
upfront and honest you can be of why
they should join and what it's actually
like to work for you and don't do the
stuff that people do which is they're

to actually win in your life and in your

staying on average 2.5 years plus but

complete transparency in the business
about who's doing what there's no

manage it the other key thing I realized
to be able to scale to this here is that
we have to really invest in our

what that means is today at G flow we
essentially have a mini Department which
we call the delivery department where we
have I think four people full-time who

to make sure everyone else is doing
their work so essentially it's like the
G flow police in the middle and they are

and because of this rigorous way of
running the business I was looking at

like onboarding a client and launching
something by 89%
and we increased by three times this

project that we were doing 12 months ago
and not only that overall because we
actually track everything we saved about
3 weeks of working time because we're
not chasing people to be like where are
you with this is this done what's
happening here which is the thing that

we didn't have a proper process. So, a

first ever started selling any products
a year and a half or something ago,
these are physical products, we weren't

each number of each item. If you have a
t-shirt that Let's just say this says uh
Supreme on it. If it's in black and
blue, and they will have three sizes
each, so that will make it like six
different SKUs. So, when the stock comes
from Let's just say whoever your
manufacturer is and you put it in your
warehouse, you've got to have some way
of knowing what what is all the items,

in, someone has to go and like pick and

has to be like, "This is what's been
bought." They have to go, find it, pick

so firstly we weren't using any
barcodes, as you can imagine that looks
to issues as people wouldn't know what's
being picked and packed exactly, cuz a
lot of things can look similar to know,
especially when it comes to like color
differences. Number two, in the process
that we noticed is that one person can't
be doing the picking and the packing.
Cuz if you were If I was to go and pick
this red t-shirt and then look at the
order saying, "Oh, this is what the
order is." and then put it in a bag,
essentially the chance of error is so
much higher because it's
it's just me doing it by myself, right?
So, we did two things. Firstly,
we changed the process so it's two
people to each order, meaning one person
picks and puts it there, the other
person then checks, "Is this the right
item? Is it the right thing?" and then
packs it. And then we introduced
barcodes, which just changed the game.
So, firstly now in in the factory each
item comes barcoded, like barcoded is
the only way. And like that, as soon as
it comes in, firstly when it comes
unloads from the truck, each item is
scanned to make sure it's the right
item. It gets stored in the right
location. So, then when the order comes
in, what happens is someone gets the
order, like, cool, I need to go and pick
these three t-shirts. They'll go on to
and it'll tell them what location it's
in, B1 location. They'll go to that
location, they scan the location B1, and
then the software that we've
added for this is like a proper
warehouse software. So, what it does is,
it's it recognizes B1, yep, correct
space.
Then it tells somebody in zone one is
t-shirt is the item. So, then they'll
scan the zone, it'll say, great, pick up
your t-shirts. And then you scan one,
two, three t-shirt, and then the tablet
on your arm says to you, correct, take
it to packing station. Then you take it
to the packing station,
and the second person is then picking it
up, rechecking the order, and is that
correct? Goes into a a mailing bag, and
then it's going towards
the third person who's organizing the
shipping that's being collected that
day. So, that's pretty much how the
system works, and
it took us a a while to really get it
right. So, what was pretty amazing is
that when we went to the guys earlier

you're like, you're doing something
right. So, that was extremely cool, and

out how things work so they don't cause

you can prioritize trying to make sure

What's going on and welcome back. And
let's dive straight into today's video
which is all about my offers, my prices,

secret. And look, the truth is it's not

to the point that I have today and we're
on track to hit $30 million of revenue
this year. Overall, the agency has done
$100 million in revenue. And my clients
combined have sold $270 million at the
time of recording this. And you know,
it's a blessing. It's a blessing to come
this far. And what's crazy in that
journey is I have paved my own way. If I
was to tell you something, I don't read
books. I don't really consume much
content. It's because of the bad
information that young agency owners are
being fed from people who don't even run
agencies. and it is starting to really
annoy me. So, I wanted to put something
together. I can promise you one thing. I
am not going to be telling you this
price, this message, this is the right
way. I'm not going to be giving you a
list of agencies to pick from, a list of
services to pick from, a list of niches
to pick from, money back guarantees that
always work. Like all the stuff that you
probably have been searching for this
whole time, that is the complete
opposite of what you should be doing.
You got to be creating everything
yourself by understanding how to. That
is the secret and that is why when I
take you through my offers you will see
what I mean. I don't care how other
people have done it before. I don't care
how someone that has you know is selling
out there as this is the best way and
there are people doing that right the
people are saying you know make it 60
leads in 60 days make it like this make
it like this make it like this and you
know you're following those methods and
you're probably not going to get many

If your business isn't growing fast

by the end of this video, I'm going to

after year all the way to 25 million
dollars last year and so far in 2026,

scale. The very first thing and the most

be built into your business. This is the
number one thing that I see with
founders that are early in their journey
is that you are only the person thinking
about growth. You don't discuss the
numbers with your team, you don't even
talk about growth and you're handling
all of that by yourself. That is the
first mistake. What you need to be doing
is actually speaking to your team about
how are we going to grow? This is what
you need to do. Add in a weekly call at
a minimum or it could even be a daily
standup for 15 minutes where you're
going to come into that call and you're
going to be like, "Guys, how are we
growing the business?" And that's going
to give you actual time to make things
happen and that is a way to force it to
actually make it happen in your
business. So, on these calls, what are

enough leads, how are we going to solve
that?

## 137. The voice in your head is keeping you stuck

The voice in your head is lying to you.
Most people think that's your instinct.
That's the real you, but it's actually
not. That voice in your head is exactly
the reason why you are where you are
today. And you know you should have been
further along in life. You know you
should have been further along with your
business because in your head you're
like, "Nah, sending like 100 emails
makes no sense. I'm not doing that. Nah,
posting content is not going to work
because it's only going to get a few
little views. Like it doesn't make
sense. That's not smart. Oh, I just need
to set up AI and it'll do it for me."
When you're saying to yourself, like,
"Hmm, no, I shouldn't do this. I should
do this instead. Oh, you know, he said
this to me, so I should like go and do
this. Oh, you know, I feel tired. I
should probably like go and
watch some Netflix now, whatever." You
keep listening to yourself. That voice
in your head lies to you all the time.
And instead of listening to it, you need
to do the opposite to it, which sounds
so crazy because for everyone that's
sacred, because that is safe. That is
because of the content you consume. That
is because of everything else you see.
And that's exactly what happens, right?
So, when you're like a bit tired, your
brain's telling you you should slow down
because that's what you've heard people
say. When things are going all over the
place, you're like, "Oh, no, I should
only do one thing." But it's like, "No,
that's the exact opposite of what I
should be doing. I need to say yes to
more things. The more I do, the more
chances to win, the more you win. Start
not trusting that voice and watch what
happens." I've been practicing this for
a long time. I say this to people all
the time, like, "What's holding you back
today?" And people don't answer it. They
don't know how to answer it. It's so
important to understand what's truly
going in your brain so you can rewire
that and start moving forward because
the truth is there's no difference
between me or you or another person or
another successful person, another
person that's done the thing you want to
do. They just didn't listen to their own
That is it.

well. So, the way I'm trying to manage
it is that I voice note to Rosie and
then she will go and type it up and send
it. So, I'm trying to like really
utilize my time better. Anyway, hope
that was helpful. I decided to make that
change, it's working well. So, yeah, I
think always be looking for a way to
improve the efficiency of how you work.
I think it doesn't matter how
successfully you are, what you've done.
I'm always looking at ways to better
myself, to make myself do things in a
better way. And at the same time, I'm
also the first one to say like I was
wrong. Um there's many things I've done.
Before I used to get the team to give me
a complete breakdown of what everyone's
in the team doing. Obviously, as people
have like a team of like six, seven,
eight, nine people, you can imagine that
um that can become very long. So, you
know, it was almost like it was not
needed. But anyway, hope you like this
video. The next few week flows we're
putting a bit more effort into. So, if

much. And I'm going to try to plan and
