## 142. This is how you GUARANTEE success (it's not talent)

The way you communicate is the single
biggest thing that's going to help you
go forward in your business or career.
So, being able to master it is the exact
thing you need to be focusing on. That
is why I'm going to show you the five
things you need to master to improve
your communication. And it's all based
on the exact same things that I've done
myself to build a business which does a
hundred million dollars plus on revenue
whilst I work with some of the biggest
brands in the world, the biggest online
entrepreneurs in the world, have
investors, and do so many things because
of my ability to communicate at
different levels and be able to execute
at the same time. So, the very first one
is that your ability to be able to speak
to the room. The truth is, one of the
best things you can learn in the art of
communication and how to present
yourself is knowing your audience and
who you are speaking with. You have to
have different versions of yourself.
Like today, I have many different
versions of myself. So, I have Shawn,
the board version, where I'm going into
board meetings and the way I need to
speak and present myself. I have Shawn
right now in terms of YouTube content
and how I need to present myself. From
the words that I choose, my tone, the
way I speak, is very different based on
the audience, based on the room that I'm
in. If it's a client, again, very
different. The niche or the industry
that you're even speaking with also
means you need to be able to tailor your
words to make them understand that you
know the industry. So, your ability to
be able to switch your style has to be
by design. It has to be thoughtful. I'll
give you an example. So, I've pitched
investors, I've raised money for my
business. So, when you're pitching
investors, any high-level corporate
people, board meetings, it's a very
different way of speaking. Because in
the corporate setting, people stay
quiet. There's nine people on the call
until one lawyer has made their point
and then you finally say, "Thanks for
that. However, blah blah blah." And you
do speak a way more professional because
you need to. But at the same time, if
I'm on a call with a client, the
client's like, "You know, I think this
is what my YouTube video should be, but
blah blah blah." I would interject and
be like, actually no, no, I've got a
better idea because I'm we're talking
way on their level, in their style
because they need to see me as someone
they can work with. The reason why a lot
of people don't sign clients is mainly
because they failed to gel because they
didn't communicate the same way. If you
look at your friends today, a lot of
your friends would be very similar to
you in terms of humor, things that they
like, the way you speak, etc. It's a bit
similar to that. And you probably would
have friends or growing up, you know,
that you didn't connect with because
they're just different to you. And
that's exactly what happens when being
an entrepreneur or a high-performance
leader, there is no option to be like,
I'm only going to work with people that
like working in this way, right? Because
you won't be able to scale as a person,
as an entrepreneur, as a business if
that was the case. So, you have to
tailor your communication style based on
the room that you're in and it's a
conscious decision. It doesn't make you
good or bad, it's just what needs to
happen in that meeting. And I do that
all the time. Number two, you have to
ask basic questions. So, if you've been
added to a meeting or you're now going
to attend a meeting with some teammates,
with some colleagues, with a boss, with
whoever, you should always take things
back to base principles and ask the most
basic questions. People think they need
to ask complex questions to sound smart,
but actually you need to do the
opposite. You should ask foundational,
fundamental questions about the thing
that is being discussed because it come
across like you are deeply trying to
understand the thing that's being
discussed. For example, we were deciding
a road map for one of our mobile apps
that we be be developing manage. I got
onto the call and they had so many
different ideas on what they want to
build. I asked a very simple question
like, what are the reviews saying today?
Well, the reviews are four out of five.
Great. Have you gone to look to all the
negative reviews and what do people
complain about? And the answer was no. I
was like, because that's what the road
map should be, right? And because
everyone was so concerned about
impressing in the meeting and come up
with their own unique ideas and AI and
all this stuff, I'm asking the most
simple basic question. They're like,
"Guys, there's people that apparently
have problems with this product. Why
have you not looked at that?" What I
said was, "What are the problems that
people say?" To which they were like,
"Oh, we actually don't know that." And I
was like, "Ah." Right? So, I just said,
"Look, before we decide what we should
do, what do we need to do? So, what are
people that have problems, what are they
saying?" Right? So, me just asking very
simple question, which to me seems
simple, but it's the right question cuz
bringing back to base principles. Like,
if you're going to improve something,
surely we need to know what's wrong with
it. Because what happens is today,
especially because of online and working
from home remote, there's a lot of
conversation about stuff. And there's
not enough clarity to be like, "Why are
we doing this?" And the other thing
that's happening a lot in businesses,
including my own today, everybody stays
busy. We all don't have any time. But,
the downside is that a lot of time gets
wasted cuz people are not focusing on
the right stuff. So, first thing about
being a leader is you want to enter a
conversation and make sure everybody's
focused on the right things. And that
happened by asking the most simple basic
questions, which will help drive the
conversation forward versus keep going
on tangents. A leader's job is to lead
people to focus on the right thing in a
conversation. It's one of the most
massive ways you can show your expertise
by actually just being very simple and
to the point. So, that's a thing about
being a leader. It's about you having
the ability to understand what's the
right thing to communicate so
everybody's moving towards something.
It's not about how senior you are, it's
not about how much you get paid, it
doesn't matter who the boss is. It's
about being the person who's leading by
design. It was the same when I I was
working as an accountant, and this was
10 years ago, and I would I did the same
thing then. I was one of the youngest
people to become like the senior
accountant at that accounting firm
because I would just come in and be
like, "Why are they spending so much
money?" And they'd be like, "Oh, like
what?" Like, again, because I'm just
asking the basic questions that need to
be asked to lead the conversation to the
right point. So, we are I'm not trying
to show off my knowledge and whatever.
And every company has those people. You
just need to stick to the basic stuff.
Number three, I would say is that you
cannot be emotional. This is about you
cannot let your emotions come across
when you communicate. And this happens
so much today because somebody did
something stressful. They're going to
call the next day and they're going to
tell everybody of like, "Oh, I was so
tired last night. Oh, it was so hard.
I'm so this." You are showcasing emotion
and that just makes people think uh
and it shows your inability to control
your emotion. One of the best skills you
can learn is to control your personal
emotion not let that come out in
communication with wider people. Now, if
there's a time and space to have such
conversations with select people, yes,
but not in just a general way. So, you
have to control your personal emotion
and not let that come out. How many
times have I been in a meeting where a
client is saying something and it's kind
of already incorrect? And I feel like
saying
you know, I have to I have to hold. I
have to compose. I have to control my
emotion because I cannot give an
emotional response. And being able to
control and not portray that is massive.
Recently, I had someone they had to deal
with a lot of issues. And and you know,
this person was being very negative in
her communication. And I had to pull
them to the side and say, "Hey, I
understand you've had a difficult 48
hours, but let's not let that become the
theme of your style of communicating to
the rest of the business cuz it's going
to impact everybody." Now, everybody's
going to feel a certain way because you
feel a certain way. And you feeling
certain way is a personal thing for you.
You cannot portray that. But that also
made me think, "Is this person really
leadership material? Could they really
be a leader? Could they really run a
department? Could they really run a
business one day?" Because if it was,
that is not a good skill to have. And I
say that for someone like I'm getting
sued right now by clients, by employees,
by whatever. But I won't let that affect
me. I won't let that come across in my
communication when I am speaking to
clients or speaking to my board,
speaking to investors, speaking to
customers because you cannot basically
showcase your emotions that you're
feeling in your communication. Number
four, and this one requires a little bit
of finesse, but ultimately, you have to
let others speak. And obviously, it's
the idea of listening to others, but
it's a little bit done differently in
business. So, you've got to empower
people to showcase their plans, showcase
their stuff, even though you may know
better. What you can't do is come into a
meeting and because you're the guy,
you're going to come in and you're going
to be like, "This is what we're doing.
We're going to do it like this." The
problem with that is that it'll become
the expected culture in your business.
So, from a communication standpoint, you
have to empower others, and you have to
let them do their thing. So, when I join
something, I would let other people
present me the plan, send me the loom,
send me the email. Like, dump everything
on me first, then I will respond with
what I think the right steps are. So,
it's very important to let other people
speak to improve your communication cuz
then when you speak, you have all the
context, all the stuff. Most leaders
think they need to lead from the front.
So, they can get on a call and, you
know, they're going to give it the whole
thing, but all along, people may have
already thought about half those things
or whatever. So, you just look like a
fool trying to be impressive, trying to
be the boss, right? And a lot of people
do that cuz of out of ego. It's the
thing that you think you need to do, but
actually, [clears throat] you need to be
the one who's letting the other person
speak, letting people present their
plans, and then moving on and giving
them a response from your point of view.
And this it will enable a culture of
like being able to do stuff and
empowering others to do things. Asking
open-ended questions in calls in the
same way. So, for example, saying like,
"What do you think we should do to do
this? Or what do you think we should do
to grow or to sign more clients? You
know, if you could change anything right
now in our business, what would you
change?" Those inviting questions
empower other people. And to be honest,
that's how I managed to find really good
team members who are actually like
young, but they became sent to the
C-suite at Jump Floor and have shares in
the business because I let them speak
for themselves. And I empowered them.
And that is a fundamental way to find
winners inside your business today. And
it's all about you setting up that
communication standard personally. The
next one is, you have to share real
insight. Because ultimately, to be the
leader, you have to be the one who the
top 10% top 1% thing has to come from
you. You have to be the one who has real
insight and tactical execution ability
in the way you communicate and in the
way you're being directive because it
has to happen. So, what you can't do, as
much as we want someone to present the
plans, your response cannot be like, I
think we should do this. It needs to be
like, we need to do this. People need
certainty. Clients need certainty. When
you are presenting investors, board,
clients, employees, at every level, you
have to be very certain in the way you
present the final thing that you are
saying we should do. After 0.1 to 4,
this final one is important because you
have to have the final say in terms of
the plan, in terms of the execution
plan, the tactics, the next steps. There
has to be absolute clarity on this is
what we're doing, this is when we need
to do it by, here's who is doing what
and how are we going to know when it
gets done, right? So, you have to be the
one driving that fully yourself. Again,
you cannot come to a meeting and just
listen and share and
brainstorm and just, you know, a lot of
chat happens, but nothing tangible comes
out of it. And to be honest, that's what
kills most businesses cuz they spend all
their time discussing stuff, but there
is nobody saying, "No, look, we're doing
this like this. We're going to get it
done like this and this is what we do."
People need someone to make the decision
and you have to be the person making the
decision in your communication. Don't
just talk, you have to set clear
actions. And if I'm being honest, the
reality is that you have to prepare. You
have to prepare going into meetings to
be like, "Okay, I think this is what we
should do. I'm going to sit and listen
to everybody else. I'm going to listen
to everybody else's plans." And then I'm
going to come with, you know, I'm going
to probably tailor what I was going to
say, but then I'm going to leave the
meeting with clear this this this this
this, right? So, I'm going to
communicate effectively and people will
know that when they're in a meeting with
me, we make decisions. When they're in a
meeting with me, we're going to have
clarity. When they have a meeting with
me, everyone's going to feel empowered.
When they have a meeting with me,
they're going to feel like, yes, we know
what we're doing next cuz ultimately,
that's what people are looking for in a
leader. They want to have that clarity
that I am helping and working alongside
someone that knows what they're doing.
And this ultimately is what's going to
make you a solid leader to be able to go
forward in your career, your business,
etc. And look, with that being said, I
post two to three videos every single
week, and make sure you hit subscribe,
and I'll see you in the next one.

## 165. You are doing too much. Start doing THIS instead.

Truth is, you're trying to do too many
different things at once. You're trying
to start a business, run a marathon, get
abs, build an online business, make
money online, travel the world, and
probably find someone to get married to.
And there's no way you can do all of
that at once, and actually win in life.
I have built a business worth $100
million plus, got married, have two
kids, and really, I've done some amazing
things in my life that I never thought
were possible. Why? As I figured out a
cheat code, a simple cheat code, which
has allowed me to do massive things and
nobody knows about. And no, it's not
monk mode. or some productivity app or a
course that teaching that I did which is
about time location freedom or a morning
routine or no scrolling before you go to
sleep. The truth is it's none of that
stuff. It's actually something that I
figured out by doing it myself and I
call it sprints. And the simple idea is
that you dedicate a certain amount of
time in your life to try to achieve one
real big thing which essentially is very
important to you or would change your
life in some capacity. So instead of
chasing a lot of things at once, you're
going to actually do them one by one and
you're really going to mean it. And to
explain this and how I've done it in my
personal life, I'm going to walk you
through three examples. So before I
actually started my business, one of the
first things I wanted to do was get
married before cuz I wanted to solve
that in my life and have that done so I
can then fully focus on growing a
business. And so unlike most people who
probably would, you know, um, get
engaged and wait years to get married
and in that time they're figuring out
what they're going to do and long-term
plans or whatever, for me, I had a very
simple sprint. The sprint was I'm going
to get engaged. I'm going to get married
and I'm going to get this done. I need
to get that part of my life done so I
can move on to the next big thing to
achieve. So from proposing to actually
getting married was only a 3-month
process because I didn't propose
thinking let's see what happens. It was
with one thing only. I'm going to
propose and from there we're going to
plan a wedding, get a wedding done and
then it's done. Which meant the thing
that people normally take 2 years on, I
dedicated the time to get it done that
much faster. Meaning naturally if you're
going to get married, you have to go
look at venues, you have to do all of
these things. But it's actually very
easy to do if that's your actual focus.
What that means though in practice is
even though of course you have to work
you have to do other things but you
start to put one thing ahead of
everything else in any time that you
have outside of your commitments that
are non-negot negotiable that makes
sense but outside of that every single
time you have goes on this one thing to
solve it you know I'm not trying to also
then start my business I'm not trying to
also do all the other things get in
shape or pick up you know hip-hop uh
street dancing because I used to do it
when I was young and you know get good
uh playing some game online and
essentially what most people try to do.
I was the same, you know, for years
growing up, I just had too many goals
and not enough progress and I was trying
to use all different methods that I
think I see other people talk about and
none of them would really work for
because I still would not actually be
doing the thing that I really need to
do. So I think in mentally that it's a
sprint and what that means is when
you're in a sprint, other things can
fall off and it's okay. So in the case
of you know when I was I need to make
sure I can get this wedding organized
other things have to fall off meaning
maybe I'm not speaking to some friends
or maybe I'm not doing something else
ultimately focusing on the key thing and
the benefit of me completing that sprint
was I was able to then launch my
business and I was already married. So
many people know in a relationship how
much time you would waste on phone
meeting up and all that stuff and you
are essentially ruining your future by
wasting your own time. So I was like I
remember actually telling my dad that
he's like why do you want to get married
now? I was like I want to get married
because you know I have plans to make
millions in my life and to do that I
need to solve this first and people were
looking at me like I'm crazy but I was
like no cuz if I don't it's going to be
something that's going to be hanging
over me and how am I going to commit to
my business and actually trying to grow
that if I'm still trying to date or
figure out when to get engaged or figure
out how to do a wedding and do all this
other stuff is not the right way to do
it. So got married, that's done. Now
let's start the business. Which is on to
my second thing, launching a business. I
did what everybody tries to do. It's a
side thing. Don't quit till you have
some customers. You know, don't leave
your job. You know, you don't want to
take a risk like that. You want to make
sure, you know, everything is good.
That's what everybody tells you. I was
the same. I was kidding myself. I would

my lunch breaks or in the evening, I
would try to do some research, try to do
some work, hustling, and you know, truth

when I decided it's time to do a sprint.
And the sprint is I'm going to launch a

it's going to work. So I left my job. No

dedicated all my time doing this one
thing. So for, you know, I gave myself
for a one year period not going to do
the things I used to do. I used to hang
out with friends. We used to go to like

and allowed myself to actually learn

course I have to spend time with my
wife, etc. you know, go to going to the
gym or whatever, right? You'll have some
non-negotiables, don't get me wrong, but

not that important whilst you're going
through that sprint, and that is fine.
For so long, I felt guilty about chasing
something on my own because I didn't go
to a family wedding, because I didn't go
to someone's birthday party. I didn't
respond to someone's message and like,
"Hey, bro, like, let's chill out,
whatever." That's exactly that one year
that I needed because when I quit my
job, it actually allowed me to quit my
job because I had the mindset of this it
has to work and it's about sitting and
focusing and getting it done. Some
practical stuff in that is things change
so much if you are doing one thing. You
go to sleep, you wake up and you're
still doing that same one thing. The
biggest reason most people are not
successful is you have, like I said, too

"No, I know what I'm doing. You know,
I'm on top of it, etc." But you still

Truth is, you can't. You can't execute

is it's not at all costs. The the

you know I signed a creator once. The

And so I was like okay I'm actually

It's new that I have to say to my, you

for me, it's like a training camp going

more productive. You know, anything that
you can improve in your actual the way
you work you should be doing that. But
don't go all in and make a bet on one
tool, one software, one this because the
landscape is changing too fast. You just
have to let it play out, be smart, and
don't fall for the hype. What I would
say is this, if someone genuinely had
configured an AI to do something which
gives them genuine advantage, they would
not be talking about it. They would not
be talking about it. If someone is
saying this tool does this, this is
this, it's purely for content, because
that guy is being like, finally,
maybe Claude bot is the way I finally
get some followers, I finally get
invited to podcasts, and that's why he's
making up an everyday and do it. There's
a guy the other day, he goes, you know,
my AI employees make my content for me
now, this stuff, whatever, and it was a
full post, and that literally was a
manual post that he typed up. And then
at the final one, it was something like
book like a call with him or something.
And I'm like,
you're saying you have AI employees and
they do this all for you, whatever, but
literally this post is written by you,
right? Like, and that's exactly what the
whole thing is happening right now.
If you look at Hicksfield, it's one of
the new tools for AI content. They don't
even use AI content in their ads.
They're getting sued, cuz they're using
real videos of other people in their
ads, so people think the software's so
amazing. And that's precisely what is
happening in the market today, and you
need to be aware of that. Every single
thing you see, most likely is a lie,
it's made up, it's whatever, because
it's so easy to make a piece of content
that's like, this changes everything,
this, this, this, this, this, and people
will jump on it. When I say people, I'm
talking about the rats in the maze that
are running around and have no idea of
how things actually work. I just want to
say that out loud, because when I say
that in private conversations, people
are like, yeah, no, I get what you mean.
If my YouTube title right now was, how
this AI software is going to change your
life, or this crazy thing is coming, and
open AI is dead, Claude is the future,
of course, that's getting a lots and
lots of clicks, but I don't want to make
that stuff, because I I don't want to
become one of the people who are in that
little rat race, and you know, enabling
it, if anything else. But anyway, that's
all I wanted to share today. It's on my
mind, it's been on my mind for a very
long time. So, So fall for the hype,
but keep using AI to make more money.
That's exactly what I'm doing.

## 170. fake gurus are the worst thing ever...

Truth is that the current state of this
online entrepreneurship space you know
you can call it money Twitter digital
money online money infospace e-commerce
drop shipping whatever right like this
space that we currently operate in it
has never been in such worse place than
it is today. It is completely filled
with the most rubbish information and
completely false thinking designed by
people that just are trying to take
advantage of young founders,
entrepreneurs, people who want to
actually do something in their life.
This whole idea that entrepreneurship is
like this escaping the system or f the
system or breaking free is far from the
truth. this idea of you're going to
start some e-commerce business and
you're going to import some stock and
sell it or drop ship it and all of a
sudden now you have location, time,
freedom, and you're going to live this
life and before you know it, you're
going to be posting your four pictures
on Twitter with your steak and your car
and your this and that's it. You've made
it. That is life. It's just
It's just not it. Look, my business did
$18 million last year. I have an agency
where I actually help brands and other
people make a lot of money online and it
is one of the hardest thing in the
world. It's taken me eight years to get
to this point. There is no such thing as
freedom. It's such a lie that it's just
wrong. And I'll tell you, look, the idea
that you have time freedom as an
entrepreneur is the opposite. It doesn't
matter what your work hours are. As a
business owner, you are on 24/7
and the time is way harder to manage
than it ever is in a job. In a job,
let's say in a normal job, you know, I
need to start something and end
something because it's designed like
that. When it's your own, it never
stops. I do calls at 2:00 a.m. with
clients. It could be 18our days with
clients. I could be flying to three
different countries to make sure clients
are happy. It never stops. And it's
always like that. Or it's 1:00 a.m. a
container has been taken over by pirates
and now I got to deal with it. That's
the reality of running a business. This
idea of like you have this freedom,
right? Do whatever you want and you're
just going to be it's, you know, living
the life of this dream is just not true.
And look, the truth is that most jobs
today, I know jobs which, you know,
online entrepreneurship people think,
ah, how can someone have a job? Most
jobs today are remote. You pretty much
don't even work 9 to5. You work from the
comfort of your house probably 4 days a
week and most places which are like
where you would be working obviously no
one's working in factories or anything
like that anymore in terms of this space
that we're talking about someone that's
watching this or in the online space you
essentially are in control of your life
in full. Most people even in my company
start the day when they want to finish
the day when they want to as long as all
the stuff is done. And most people are
focusing and doing things they actually
want to do in life. That is freedom
because you are choosing to do what you
want to do. This idea that you've got to
leave your job, leave uh some education
and you got to go and start a drop
shipping store or an agency or something
else and that's freedom. It's just not.
Also, it is so hard to be successful in
any one of those businesses that most
guys online are telling you to start
because the amount of skills that you
need to have to actually make a success
of it makes it hard. Makes it the
opposite of freedom. makes it complex,
makes it stressful, makes it extremely
hard to succeed in. So, business is not
easy. It is very, very tough. And look,
it's not that it doesn't work. Of
course, it does. It's one of the best
things in the world to start your own
business like I have and make it into a
success. But if you're starting a
business thinking it's freedom, thinking
you're escaping the system, this same
system that apparently you're escaping,
it's it's so funny because ultimately,
what is it, right? If you work for
someone today, let's say you're an
agency, you work for an agency, a client
is paying the agency, the agency pays
you, right? You're in the system. Ah,
you know, all my days this can't be the
case. I have to escape it. What's the
difference now? The a client is just
going to pay you directly. There's no
real change. What's changed about your
life? Your money is still in a bank.
You're still probably leasing a car.
You're still paying rent. What system
have you escaped?
Right. It's the stupidest thing and kids
everywhere are like, I need to do this.
I need to start my own business. I need
to do this. And it's just Yeah. Anyway,
I'm tired of seeing it because when I'm
online, I'm always looking for relevant
business content that will actually help
me and you just can't find it because
most things are just all geared towards
this one thing now, which is live the
life of freedom. Do this, this, this.
You're going to be living like me. Look
how much money I have. Look be and so
you want to be like me. Oh, this is how
to do it. This business model is the
best and that is it. And look, that
brings me to the second thing that I
wanted to talk about, which is this
fascination with 10K a month. It is the
most funniest thing. So, you know, any
business owners that have done seven
figures plus in their business or have
been doing this for a long time,
essentially someone like myself and
other colleagues that I have, we laugh
at that stuff because it's the most
corniest and cheesiest thing in the
world. When someone's like, "How do you
get to 10K a month, bro?" It's just
like, what are you talking about?
There's so many things that are just
insanely wrong with this whole 10K
movement, that fascination movement,
whatever. First thing is that anyone
that's making content about 10K a month,
they won't have an actual business,
which is their primary source of
revenue, which is actually something
else. The way they make their money is
essentially teaching other people, you
how to make 10K per month. So the only
people in the world that talk about 10k
a month are the people that essentially
are telling you how to make 10k per
month. Meaning no actual business owners
that have done something else than just
teach people how to make 10k per month
talk about 10k per month which just
shows how wrong or messed up the whole
thing is. Ultimately, some young people
who've realized who are great at
marketing, great at creating content,
figured it out and essentially keep
telling other people, "This is how you
make money, by the way. Pay me. I'm
going to tell you how to make money."
And the whole time, the only way they've
ever made money is by telling you how to
make money. That's the irony of it. But
for some reason, nobody sees this.
People are still like, "Oh, no. I love
following that guy or he knows what he's
talking about because this is the best
way to make 10K per month." It just
makes no sense. The other thing that
extremely infuriates me is that the
tactics that these 10K a month
entrepreneurs deploy to actually sell
is completely immoral, unethical,
and just Why? Because people
will use emotional blackmail to try to
sell to people, which is just we
cannot be doing. But the truth is you
get away with it because it's the online
space and there's no rules. You can do
whatever. What I mean by that is
like this. I've seen the sales
scripts. I actually for fun sometimes go
into people's funnels and I see what
they're saying, what they're doing. I've
even got on a point with setting cold
call sales calls and it's just amazing
to me. I even get my team to do it
sometimes cuz I'm always I've been so
intrigued by what are people doing it?
How are people convincing people that
they should leave their job and start a
drop shipping store and they're going to
have a rolly going to have a whip and
going to be living this led lifestyle.
So, I was like, you know, let me see.
And it's crazy. People will use tactics
like you get on a sales call and it'll
be like, are you the decision maker?
Okay. Do you have a card with you? Okay.
And at every point, they will use
emotional blackmail to be like, do you
not want to make enough money? Do you
not want to buy a house for your mom?
You know, what type of person do you
want to be? Do you want to have a
legacy? Do you want to have this? They
use every tactic to convince someone to
buy the course, to buy the thing that
they're selling us and get them to 10K
per month. And it's crazy. It's just not
okay, right? Because what are we doing?
We're essentially it's you can't use the
word scam, right? Because ultimately
there is content, there is going to be
some value, there is going to be
something. It's just unethical. It's
immoral. It's just the way you are
selling, the tactics that you're using
is just not the right. And I don't like
it. And I don't want to say it because
it's been on my mind. I see it all the
time. And I wish more younger people,
look, I'm 30 plus. I've been doing this
for a long time. I wish more younger
people saw through that Like
if someone can really help you out and
they have an amazing product, go buy it.
But most of the times people are selling
you something and you should know that
the moment you see a title on YouTube
saying something 10K per month or a
tweet or a LinkedIn post, just know
that's the start of their funnel and at
some point in that journey, they're
going to use every tactic possible to
get you to give your money to them. As
long as you know that, then it's cool.
But it's important because most people
don't know. When you go through your
first funnel experience or a sales call
experience, you are unaware. And in most
cases, you are going to get done over
because before you know it, you've paid
$8,000 because you're trying to get to
your 10K per month. They're giving you
some guarantee. You know, it's all going
to work out, but you still paid the
money. The terms and conditions are
going to make sure that you never get
that money back. And that's the truth.
What I wanted to get off my chest was
that if you're starting a business in
the wrong way with the wrong thought
process with the wrong inspiration, it's
going to fail because you are thinking
that I'm going to get rich fast. Even
though it's not a get-rich quick scheme,
of course it's not, right? That's what
everybody says. But what they actually
are saying to you is, "Hey, you want to
be like me? Just do this. I'm giving it
to you all." And before you know it, you
got the rolly and you're going to be
loving life. Starting my own business
has changed my life. I would love for
anyone to start a business and change
their lives. Just know it's going to be
hard. It's going to be a million times
harder than any job will ever be. The
financial pressure of knowing you've got
to do all the work. You got to pay a
bunch of people and then you finally pay
yourself is so much harder than getting
paid at the end of the month from a job
that you're at. I know that because I
worked I had a job for years. Then I
left. I started my own business. is
today I employ 100 people. I have to
make sure we make enough money to pay
the 100 people and all software and
everything else and rent in four offices
around the world and have enough profit
left over and pay myself. That is way
harder than me right now. I could
probably go and work for I don't know
Instagram as a creator director and get
paid a salary. It'll be way easier and I
think I'll have way more freedom in my
life and it's just that. But you are
playing for the big upside, right? Why
am I doing this? Why should you start a
business? You should start a business in
the right way knowing that the next 3 to
5 years of your life are going to be the
hardest years of your life. But if you
do it for the right reasons, if you do
it with the right thinking, the right
mentality, you will find success. And if
you were to, you will build equity value
for yourself. Right? And if you look at
any successful person in the world, how
did they get rich? They didn't get rich
by making a lot of cash per month.
Anyone that's ever gone rich is because
they built something and sold it. From
Elon Musk, from PayPal to anyone else
you want to look at in the history or
any major entrepreneurs online that are
sophisticated, real
entrepreneurs. They built their wealth
by building businesses and selling them.
So that is why you should start a
business. You should start a business to
build enough value that someone will
want to buy it off you one day. Whether
you want to sell it or not, you know,
your decision. Whether you want to live
off that cash flow forever, your
decision. Live off the dividends, your
decision. Or you want to sell it. That
is the way to wealth. That is the why
you should start a business to do it for
the right reasons in the right way. And
you can make a lot of money, but just
know it's going to be extremely hard
work. And look, I took a break from
making content because I've been busy
building my business, which over the
past few years I have massively grown.

UK. You'll see we did £15 million, which

honestly what it takes to build a
business, tactics, actual things that I
go through building my business and
everything else. And I promise you,

thing that it is. It's hard, but also

things. There's one thing that the whole

is the fun of actually achieving stuff.
So, I released a drink called Newtonic,
hit number one on Amazon. I released an

## 172. the BRUTAL truth about being an entrepreneur...

I left my job 9 years ago and in that
time I've built a business which has
built more than $100 million. But in
this video I'm not going to tell you how
you can achieve that or I'm going to
tell you a big success story or anything
like that. I'm going to do the opposite.
I'm going to tell you the dark side and
the real truth of building a business
which essentially has done 9 figures in
revenue. Because even though this was my
dream, even though when I was working
and the dream was to quit the 9 to-5 and
